text.title: Onboarding Checklist by Phase
text.summary: Verifiable action items for each onboarding phase from before Day 1 through month 1, with completion criteria.

section.title: Before Day 1

rule.statement: All seven preboarding items must be complete before sending the Day-1 schedule to the new hire.
rule.rationale: Sending a Day-1 schedule when equipment has not shipped or accounts are not provisioned sets up a failed first day and damages trust in the organization.

list.item: Equipment ordered and confirmed shipped with tracking number.
Email, Slack, and primary tools accounts created and verified.
Welcome email sent with first-week schedule and point-of-contact name.
Team notified of start date, role, and brief intro bio.
Buddy assigned and briefed on scope and first-week check-in date.
Benefits enrollment instructions sent.
Manager has blocked calendar for Day-1 1:1 and week-1 daily check-ins.

section.reference: Automated gate: run 5 days before start; blocks welcome email if any item is RED.

list.item: Warm welcome from team within first 30 minutes.
IT setup verified complete (laptop on, tools accessible).
Paperwork and benefit enrollment signed.
Manager 1:1 completed: expectations discussed, first project scope shared.
Introduction to buddy completed.
All recurring key meetings (standups, 1:1s, team syncs) added to calendar.
Access to all required systems verified by the new hire.
Training modules assigned with completion dates.
Week-1 pulse survey sent (4 questions, anonymous).
Introduction to key stakeholders outside immediate team completed.
30-day check-in completed with manager; goals for days 31-60 documented.
Feedback collected and blockers addressed.
Day-30 detailed onboarding survey sent.
Buddy check-in: confirm buddy program is active and useful.

rule.statement: 30-day goals must be documented in a shared artifact before the check-in closes; verbal agreements decay within a week.
rule.rationale: Undocumented goals cannot be referenced at the day-60 review; the hire enters phase 2 without a written contract of what success looks like.

antipattern.description: Documentation-as-onboarding: assigning a new hire a reading list without synchronous context.
antipattern.reason: New hires do not read documentation as a primary learning mode; it is a reference, not an onboarding mechanism. An agent piping docs is a worse experience than a human explanation.

antipattern.description: Buddy program with no check-in cadence or scope definition.
antipattern.reason: Buddy relationships degrade silently when buddies stop checking in; without a scheduled first meeting and defined scope, the buddy becomes a name on a form.
